title: The Confessions of St. Augustine
author: Augustine, of Hippo, Saint
summary: Augustine's Confessions Book I is a prayerful interior monologue in which the narrator addresses God, praising divine greatness, wrestling with how finite humans can call on an infinite Creator, and confessing personal sinfulness. He recalls infancy and dependence on mother and nurses, the formation of desires, childish indignation and envy, and questions about memory, being, and God's presence everywhere. Augustine pleads for cleansing, enlargement of his narrow soul, and divine mercy. He meditates on God's attributes—omnipotence, mercy, justice— and acknowledges human restlessness until the heart finds repose in God, whose mercy and providence shaped his life and invites humble praise.

characters.name: Augustine (narrator)
characters.personality: introspective, penitential, philosophical, longing for God
characters.lifespan: living in the present narrative; remembers infancy and seeks divine mercy

characters.name: God / The Lord
characters.personality: omnipotent, merciful, just, incomprehensible as described by the narrator
characters.lifespan: eternal; sovereign presence throughout the narrative

characters.name: Mother
characters.lifespan: appears as Augustine's biological mother who nursed him—no further fate given in the excerpt

characters.name: Nurses
characters.personality: compassionate, caring, instrumental in infant care
characters.lifespan: present in Augustine's infancy as caregivers; no further fate given

characters.name: Parents of my flesh
characters.personality: providers of life and early care
characters.lifespan: receive Augustine in infancy (biological parents); no further fate detailed

characters.name: The Preacher
characters.personality: ministerial, instrumental in transmitting faith and doctrine
characters.lifespan: preaches the Incarnation and shapes Augustine's faith formation in the narrative

characters.name: The Son (Incarnation of Thy Son)
characters.personality: mediator referenced as part of salvation history
characters.lifespan: eternal divine person, mentioned as incarnate in the preaching that reached Augustine

characters.name: Elders
characters.personality: authoritative but not always submissive to infant will; represent social order
characters.lifespan: figure in Augustine's childhood memories; no detailed fate given

characters.name: Foster-brother
characters.personality: rival in infancy; object of envy in Augustine's memory
characters.lifespan: appears in a remembered incident of infant envy; no further fate given

characters.name: The Enemy
characters.personality: spiritual adversary from whom Augustine asks to be spared
characters.lifespan: mentioned as a power from which Augustine seeks deliverance

characters.name: Women with child
characters.personality: examples Augustine observes when pondering pre-birth existence
characters.lifespan: cited in memory/observation; no personal fates given
